weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a polished blue sky, the sun already purposeful and clean-edged, lighting up verandas and gum leaves with a gentle glare. Forecasts hold that same clarity through late morning into early afternoon, so expect unbroken sunshine and that sharp, glassy visibility that makes colors snap. The UV will climb quickly toward very high around midday; shade will matter as much as comfort.

It looks like one of those straightforward, good-natured days: warming as the hours stretch on, dry air and no hint of change. Dress light and breathable, with a brimmed hat, sunglasses, and generous sunscreen; carry water if you’re out past lunchtime. This is a fine morning for unhurried errands on foot, a coffee at a quiet table beneath an awning, or getting the washing on the line to drink in the sun. Around noon, slip indoors for a spell—browse a bookshop, visit a gallery, or catch up at the library—and then, when the light softens, take a short wander through the neighborhood’s leafy streets.
